title: Development News: Satellite Infrastructure Overhaul, Lua API, and Modular Rocket Engines
author: Webyep
summary:
Lately, more and more players want to create fully functioning orbital networks capable of gathering, processing, and transmitting data, rather than just decorative satellites. Therefore, we have started a major overhaul of the entire satellite infrastructure.

### Radio Channel

The mod will feature a dedicated communication radio channel.

This will be a data stream independent of Minecraft, which will continue to operate regardless of what happens in the game world and what dimension the player is in. Thanks to this, satellites, rockets, stations, and other devices will be able to exchange information without the usual limitations of distance and dimensions.

In fact, this will become the foundation for building real communications, telemetry, and remote control networks.

### Overhauling Satellites

The current satellite system will receive one of its biggest changes since the mod's inception.

Previously, the logic of standard nodes was implemented directly in Java. This worked, but it made the system less flexible, complicated changes, and periodically led to errors that had to be fixed at the mod level itself.

Now, we are completely moving away from this approach.

All standard nodes will be rewritten in Lua. Their logic will no longer be hardcoded in Java and will become part of Lua scripts.

This means players will not only be able to use ready-made nodes but also study their structure, modify existing behaviors, and create custom solutions for their tasks.

Along with this, Lua will receive a significantly expanded API for working with satellites, data transmission, peripherals, and other systems of the mod.

Our goal is to make the satellite system as open, expandable, and independent of the mod's internal implementation as possible.

### The Return of Peripherals

Peripheral devices for satellite engines are also returning to the mod, but in a completely new form.

Previously, the interaction was built around coordinates and searching for the nearest blocks. This approach scaled poorly and created many limitations.

Now, each peripheral block will receive its own unique identifier and will be able to provide detailed information about itself.

Furthermore, you no longer need to install the satellite directly on the rocket. It will be enough to link the devices together.

Data transfer between them will also occur via the radio channel, which essentially removes distance limitations and allows building distributed control systems.

### What does this mean for contributors?

Fortunately or unfortunately, the project is currently undergoing a deep refactoring.

Some systems were created in the early stages of development, when many mechanics were not yet fully thought out. Certain architectural decisions were made based on current tasks and over time began to limit the project's development.

Therefore, it is now important to rework the fundamental parts of the system while it can still be done without serious consequences.

Yes, some internal APIs will change. Yes, some existing code may require adaptation. But this is a necessary stage if we want to continue developing the mod and not run into the limitations of the old architecture.

The goal of refactoring is to create a codebase that will allow us to implement new mechanics faster, maintain existing systems more easily, and reduce the number of bugs in the future.

### What's next?

In addition to architectural changes, several major gameplay systems are in development.

#### Modular Rocket Engines

Many of you might have already seen them in early teasers and videos.

A full-fledged modular engine system is being added to the game, where the engine's characteristics will be determined by its design rather than a single block.

The engine will consist of three main components:

* Thruster Mount - contains fuel and coolant reserves and is responsible for powering the propulsion system.
* Engine Pipes - determine the engine's operating cycle.
* Engine Nozzle - the final element of the design, affecting thrust and heat dissipation.

Different types of pipelines will change the engine characteristics:

* Closed Cycle - a balanced option.
* Open Cycle - increased thrust at the cost of increased resource consumption.
* Full-Flow Cycle - maximum performance, but high heating.
* Expander Cycle - high efficiency and low fuel consumption, but less thrust.

Nozzles will also differ in characteristics. For example, titanium nozzles will be able to produce more thrust and handle heat more efficiently.

During operation, the engine will consume fuel and coolant, generate heat, create physical thrust for the ship, and render a full-fledged jet exhaust plume.

This way, players will be able to design propulsion systems for specific tasks, rather than just choosing an engine based on the "better or worse" principle.

#### Other Planned Systems

* Data-driven universe system.
* Precision landing system for celestial bodies.
* Blocks for working with orbital directions:
  * Prograde
  * Retrograde
  * Normal
  * Antinormal
  * Radial Out
  * Radial In
* Time Warp in survival mode.
* Copycat Separator, Encased Copycat Separator, and Separator Shaft for multi-stage rockets and transmitting rotation between stages.

### A question for the community

A possible change to the space part of the world is also currently being discussed.

One option is to abandon the zero-gravity zone between 5,000 and 20,000 blocks in the normal world and replace it with asteroids containing levitite cores, which will create local areas of zero-gravity.

What do you think about this?
